reasoning: Shantanu Thakur is still the only one who actually brought CAA into law—not just words, but actual policy. Yes, the papers are moving slowly, my parents still don't have them, and these fourteen certificates in five days look more like a show than real delivery. But switching to TMC? They had thirty years and did nothing, and now they think any candidate will convince us. At least Shantanu kept his word, at least our community has someone in Delhi fighting. I have to trust that the process will move, even if it's slower than I hoped. The promise is law now—that much I know is real, even if my parents' actual papers haven't come through yet.
primary_drivers: CAA is law—Shantanu Thakur delivered what seemed impossible in 2019
Matua community has political power for the first time; switching away would mean betraying that
TMC's thirty-year indifference and contempt for our identity, shown by running Biswajit Das instead of a real Matua
